etymology.description: 言(言) (yán, "words") + 八(八) (bā, "breath coming from the mouth") + 厃(危) (wěi, "danger") -> speak too much
etymology.interpretation: The character 詹(詹) (zhān) combines 言(言) (yán, "words") to indicate speech, 八(八) (bā, "breath coming from the mouth") to represent the act of speaking, and 厃(危) (wěi, "danger") to suggest excessiveness or danger when speaking too much, ultimately conveying the idea of excessive talk or speaking beyond what is proper.
